Package recoder.convenience

Class Naming

java.lang.Object
recoder.convenience.Naming
public abstract class Naming extends Object
Utility class to obtain or transform Identifiers obeying a set of naming conventions. There should be distinguishable Identifiers for features, constants and classes for any given base name.

The default Java naming scheme defines lower case first letters for features, upper case first letters for classes, lower case for packages, and upper case for constants (final attributes).

Examples:
createFeatureName("HelloWorld").equals("helloWorld")
createClassName("helloWorld").equals("HelloWorld")
createPackageName("HelloWorld").equals("helloworld")
createConstantName("HelloWorld").equals("HELLOWORLD")

All methods create new String or Identifier objects.

    • makeFilename

      public static String makeFilename(String logicalName)
      Makes a valid system-specific file path name from a logical (dotted) name. For instance, "java.lang" is converted into "java" + File.separatorChar + "lang".

    • dot

      public static String dot(String prefix, String suffix)
      Concatenates two strings with a dot in the middle. Null strings are considered empty. The implementation is faster than the usual "+" translation and slightly faster than StringBuffer operations and it also needs less memory.

    • toArrayTypeName

      public static String toArrayTypeName(String basename, int dimensions)
      Returns the name of an array type with the given basename and the given number of dimensions. If dimensions are zero or the basename is null, the basename is returned. Example: toArrayTypeName("java.lang.Object", 2) yields "java.lang.Object[][]".

    • toPathName

      public static String toPathName(ReferencePrefix ref)
      Creates the dotted path name for a reference prefix. Returns an empty string if the prefix is not a NameReference. Appends array brackets if the prefix is a type reference with a dimension > 0.
      Parameters:
      ref - a reference prefix.
      Returns:
      the dotted representation of the given reference.

    • toPathName

      public static String toPathName(ReferencePrefix ref, String suffix)
      Creates the dotted path name for a reference prefix and appends a suffix string. Returns the suffix if the prefix is not a NameReference. Ignores the suffix if it is null or empty. Assumes that the prefix is not a type reference with a dimension > 0.
      Parameters:
      ref - a reference prefix.
      suffix - a suffix string.
      Returns:
      the dotted representation of the given reference and the suffix.
      Since:
      0.72

    • getPackageName

      public static String getPackageName(CompilationUnit cu)

    • toCanonicalName

      public static String toCanonicalName(CompilationUnit cu)
      Derives the canonical name for a compilation unit. Combines the name of the package and the name of the primary type declaration. If the primary type declaration is not public, or no type is declared within the compilation unit, it's attempted to keep the old filename instead.
      Parameters:
      cu - a compilation unit.
      Returns:
      the canonical name of the unit.

    • toCanonicalFilename

      public static String toCanonicalFilename(CompilationUnit cu)
      Derives the canoncial file name for a compilation unit. Combines the name of the package and the name of the primary type declaration, transforms it into a platform specific file name and appends the ".java" filename suffix.
      Parameters:
      cu - a compilation unit.
      Returns:
      the canonical file name of the unit.

    • getFullName

      public static String getFullName(ClassTypeContainer ct, String suffix)

    • getFullName

      public static String getFullName(ClassTypeContainer ct)

    • getFullName

      public static String getFullName(Field f)

    • isKeyword

      public static boolean isKeyword(String name)
      Checks if the given name is a Java keyword and hence an invalid identifier.
      Parameters:
      name - the name to check.
      Returns:
      true if the given name is a keyword, false otherwise.
